summary: First cold spec-judge dispatch returned blocked on the mechanical expected-subset governance pre-flight: the relayed inline spec-producer governances_loaded omitted sdd:lifecycle-governance and sdd:gate-validation-governance from the declared set, the two that adjudicate the CR's own freeze/no-re-open/no-status-transition claim. Loaded both governance bodies at the gate and re-verified the determination against them (empty .feature diff -> freeze intact, stronger than additive-self-clears; node README carries no lifecycle field so it is legal per the per-node spec-type checks; root spec stays implemented, no status transition). Re-declared the full seven-governance set and re-dispatched the spec-judge. Content facts the judge gathered pre-short-circuit were all clean.

Resolved the backlog followup in-session on the user's 'just fix it': the exit-code ordering corner is now DECIDED as backend-unsupported (exit 1) wins over missing-pane (exit 2). Rationale: a geometry-incapable backend cannot be captured for any pane, so --from cannot rescue it — telling the caller to pass --from (exit 2) is a dead-end hint. cli.ts now raises the backend-unsupported refusal BEFORE target resolution, throwing the library's CaptureUnsupportedError (mapped to exit 1 in the single backendUnsupported site) so the decision stays single-source; the derive orchestrators keep their own member check as their library contract. Pinned by an ADDITIVE frozen scenario template-capture-backend-refusal-outranks-missing-pane in cli/template/capture (widens the contract, narrows nothing -> freeze self-clears, no re-open; a detail-adjustment folded under conductor authority) plus a binding test. cli/template/capture now 15/15 BOUND+PASS (was 14); template/capture unchanged at 21/21. pnpm verify green. The followup is thus resolved, not filed — nothing outstanding to drain.
